title: Cleaning process for a powder transfer system
abstract: Method of cleaning a powder transfer system (100) including providing a powder transfer system having a washing fluid source (128), a spray device valve (140) in fluid communication with the washing fluid source, a backflush valve (132) in fluid communication with the washing fluid source, a receiving device (112) having upper (122) and lower (124) chambers, and a membrane filter (126) disposed between the upper and lower chambers. The method includes opening the backflush valve to initiate and run a backflush cycle to wash the upper chamber with fluid, and closing the backflush valve after the backflush cycle completes. The method includes opening the spray device valve to initiate and run a spray device cycle to wash the lower chamber with fluid, and closing the spray device valve after the spray device cycle completes. The method includes opening a pressure valve (148) to provide PCA to the receiving container.
claims:
Claims What is Claimed: 1. A method of cleaning a complex powder transfer system, the method comprising: providing a powder transfer system including a washing fluid source, a spray device valve in fluid communication with the washing fluid source, a backflush valve in fluid communication with the washing fluid source, a receiving device having an upper chamber in fluid communication with the backflush valve and a lower chamber in fluid communication with the spray device valve, and a membrane filter disposed between the upper and lower chambers; opening the backflush valve to initiate a backflush cycle; running the backflush cycle to wash the upper chamber with a fluid from the washing fluid source; closing the backflush valve after the backflush cycle completes; opening the spray device valve to initiate a spray device cycle; running the spray device cycle to wash the lower chamber with a fluid from the washing fluid source, the lower chamber being washed separately from the upper chamber; closing the spray device valve after the spray device cycle completes; and opening a pressure valve to provide a processed control air (PCA) to the receiving container. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein running the backflush cycle includes running a first step of the backflush cycle by providing ambient water from the washing fluid source at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein running the backflush cycle includes running a second step after running the first step, wherein running the second step includes providing a clean-in-place (CIP) solution from the washing fluid source to the upper chamber. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein running the second step includes providing the CIP solution at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein running the backflush cycle includes running a third step of the backflush cycle after running the second step, wherein running the third step includes providing a second CIP solution from the washing fluid source to the upper chamber, and wherein the second CIP solution has a different acidity than the first CIP solution. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein running the third step includes providing the second CIP solution at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 through 6, wherein running the backflush cycle includes running a final step by providing water from the washing fluid source to the upper chamber. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein running the final step includes providing hot water at a temperature in a range between approximately 75 degrees Celsius and approximately 90 degrees Celsius and at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi. 9. The method of claim 1 , wherein running the backflush cycle includes running at least four backflush cycles, wherein the washing fluid in a first step is water and the washing fluid in a second step is a CIP solution. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 through 9, wherein opening the pressure valve includes opening the pressure valve after running the backflush cycle and before running the spray device cycle. 1 1 . The method of any one of claims 1 through 10, wherein opening the pressure valve includes opening the pressure valve before opening the spray device valve such that the lower chamber is sprayed with cleaning fluid while PCA blows in the lower chamber 12. The method of any one of claims 1 through 1 1 , further comprising providing an air barrier between the membrane filter and the lower chamber during the spray device cycle. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 through 12, wherein running the spray device cycle includes running a first step of the spray device cycle by spraying water into the lower chamber. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein running the first step of the spray device cycle includes spraying ambient water into the lower chamber at a pressure in a range between approximately 40 psi and approximately 50 psi. 15. The method of claim 13 or 14, wherein running the first step of the spray device cycle includes running the first step of the spray device cycle after the first step of the backflush cycle completes. 16. The method of any one of claims 13 through 15, wherein running the spray device cycle includes running a second step of the spray device cycle after running the first step of the spray device cycle, the second step includes spraying a CIP solution from the washing fluid source into the lower chamber. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein running the second step of the spray device cycle includes spraying the CIP solution into the lower chamber at a pressure in a range between approximately 40 psi and approximately 50 psi. 18. The method of claim 16 or 17, wherein running the second step of the spray device cycle includes running the second step of the spray device cycle after the second step of the backflush cycle completes and before the third step of the backflush cycle initiates. 19. The method of any one of claims 16 through 18, wherein running the spray device cycle includes running a third step of the spray device cycle after running the second step of the spray device cycle, the third step of the spray device cycle includes spraying a second CIP solution into the lower chamber, the second CIP solution having a different acidity than the first CIP solution. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein running the third step includes spraying the second CIP solution into the lower chamber at a pressure in a range between approximately 40 psi and approximately 50 psi. 21 . The method of claim 19, wherein running the third step of the spray device cycle includes running the third step of the spray device cycle after the third step of the backflush cycle completes and before the final step of the backflush cycle initiates. 22. The method of any one of claims 15 through 21 , wherein running the spray device cycle includes running a final step of the spray device cycle after running the third step of the spray device cycle, the final step including spraying water into the lower chamber. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein running the final step includes spraying hot water at a temperature in a range between approximately 75 degrees Celsius and approximately 90 degrees Celsius and into the lower chamber at a pressure in a range between approximately 40 psi and approximately 50 psi. 24. A method of cleaning a big bag station of a powder transfer system, the method comprising: providing a big bag station including a bag body, a washing fluid source coupled to the bag body, an agitator coupled to the bag body, a first valve in fluid communication with the washing fluid source and the bag body, a second valve in fluid communication with a pressure source and the bag body, and a drain valve; running a prewash cycle by opening the first, second, and drain valves and providing a washing fluid via the washing fluid source to the bag body; closing first, second, and drain valves after the prewash cycle is complete; operating an agitator; running a bag body wash cycle by opening the first valve to fill the bag body with a washing fluid, and closing the first valve after the bag body is filled with the washing fluid; agitating the washing fluid in the bag body; and 

 draining the washing fluid from the bag body by opening the drain valve. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein running the bag body wash cycle includes running a first step of the bag body wash cycle by providing a CIP solution via the washing fluid source into the bag body through the first valve, closing the first valve, agitating the CIP solution in the bag body, and opening the drain valve when the first step is complete. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein running the first step includes providing the CIP solution into the bag body at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi and agitating the CIP solution. 27. The method of claim 25 or 26, wherein running the bag body wash cycle includes running a second step of the bag body wash cycle after the first step, the second step includes providing a different CIP solution from the washing fluid source into the bag body through the first valve, closing the first valve, agitating the different CIP solution in the bag body, and opening the drain valve when the second step is complete. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein running the second step includes providing the different CIP solution into the bag body at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi. 29. The method of claim 27 or 28, wherein running the bag body wash cycle includes running a third step of the bag body wash cycle after the second step, the third step includes providing water from the washing fluid source into the bag body through the first valve, closing the first valve, agitating the water in the bag body, and opening the drain valve when the third step is complete. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein running the third step includes providing the water into the bag body at a pressure in a range between approximately 45 psi and approximately 50 psi. 31. The method of any one of claims 24 through 30, further comprising opening the pressure valve to eject the washing fluid from the bag body. 32. The method of claim 31 , further comprising operating a vacuum to pull the washing fluid through the pressure valve, wherein the vacuum is operating at full capacity. 33. A method of cleaning a complex powder transfer system, the method comprising: providing a powder transfer system including a washing fluid source, a spray device valve in fluid communication with the washing fluid source, a backflush valve in fluid communication with the washing fluid source, a receiving device having an upper chamber in fluid communication with the backflush valve and a lower chamber in fluid communication with 

 the spray device valve, and a membrane filter disposed between the upper and lower chambers; opening the backflush valve to initiate a backflush cycle; running the backflush cycle to wash the upper chamber with a fluid from the washing fluid source, the backflush cycle including four steps; closing the backflush valve after the backflush cycle completes; opening the spray device valve to initiate a spray device cycle, the spray device cycle including four steps; running the spray device cycle to wash the lower chamber with a fluid from the washing fluid source, the lower chamber being washed separately from the upper chamber; closing the spray device valve after the spray device cycle completes; alternating the four steps of the spray device cycle with the four steps of the backflush cycle; and opening a pressure valve to provide a processed control air (PCA) to the receiving container.
